disclaimer: Pilot data for governance review support only. Not legal advice. Not a compliance guarantee. Not complete global coverage. Sample records require human review before client use. Official sources control.

items.name: Australia
items.notes: Curated federal and national regulator sources. Does not cover all state/territory laws or sector regulators unless added later. Requires human review.

items.name: Canada
items.notes: Federal and national-authority sources only. Does not include all provincial or territorial AI or privacy regimes unless added later. Requires human review.

items.name: China
items.notes: Curated entry for official cyber/data/AI governance sources where listed. Does not claim complete coverage of PRC law, provincial rules, or all CAC publications. Many materials are in Chinese; human review and language expertise required.

items.name: European Union
items.notes: Pilot jurisdiction for Caesar AI Regulation Watch. Tracks a curated subset of official and authoritative EU sources that helps identify regulatory changes relevant to AI governance review. Does not include all EU institutions, member-state implementations, or sector-specific rules unless added later. Changes surfaced here may affect controls or evidence only after human review; requires human review before client use.

items.name: G7
items.notes: Intergovernmental process entry for G7 Hiroshima AI Process official materials. Does not create binding law; national implementation varies. Not exhaustive of all G7 work.

items.name: Japan
items.notes: Entry for identifiable official government and METI AI governance sources. Does not include all ministries, standards bodies, or local rules unless added later.

items.name: Norway
items.notes: Pilot jurisdiction for Caesar AI Regulation Watch. Norway is an EEA country; EU AI Act-related obligations may apply via EEA incorporation on a different timeline than EU member states. This registry tracks selected official Norwegian sources only—not all ministries, agencies, or sector regulators. Norway is not part of the EU; related_frameworks (eu, eea) indicate where EEA/EØS incorporation of EU rules may be relevant for monitoring context only—not a legal determination. Supports governance review; may affect controls or evidence after human review. Requires human review before client use.

items.name: OECD
items.notes: International organisation entry for OECD AI Policy Observatory and related official publications. Does not substitute for national law in member countries.

items.name: Singapore
items.notes: Curated official sources for Singapore AI governance and related digital policy. Not exhaustive of all statutes, sector rules, or MAS guidance unless added later.

items.name: South Korea
items.notes: Curated official government and PIPC-related sources where listed. Not complete coverage of ROK legislation or all regulators. Requires human review.

items.name: United Kingdom
items.notes: Expanded static coverage for curated UK government and regulator sources on AI policy and guidance. Does not include devolved administrations, sector regulators beyond listed sources, or all UK legislation. Requires human review before client use.

items.name: UNESCO
items.notes: International body entry for UNESCO AI ethics recommendation and related official pages. Soft-law orientation; not binding national regulation. Human review required.

items.name: United States (federal)
items.notes: Federal-level AI regulation and policy monitoring entry only. Does not cover US state or local AI laws, all federal agencies, or sector-specific rules unless added later. Requires human review before client use.
